description: The whole design as one portable document — every node and edge, sorted so two exports of an unchanged graph are byte-identical. Use it to back the design up, move it between machines, or migrate it across a reflow2 upgrade (export with the old build, import with the new). It carries a stamp saying which reflow2 wrote it. Pass `path` to write the document to a file instead of returning it — on a large design the payload overflows what a session can read, and a backup wants to be a file anyway.

overwrite.description:
Allow `path` to replace an existing file. Off by default: an export
writes freely to a new path but refuses to clobber an existing one
unless you say so, so a stray or injected path cannot silently destroy
a file (BL-57).

path.description:
Write the export to this file (deterministic sorted-key JSON, diffable
under git) and return only {path, bytes, nodes, edges, content_hash,
prev_content_hash, stamp}. Replacing an existing export links the new
document to the old one's content hash (lineage; chain advances only
when content changed). Omit to get the whole document as the result
payload.

name: export_graph
